Question: Define a point.
Answer: A point is an exact location in space; it has no length

Question: Define a line.
Answer: A line is a straight one-dimensional figure extending infinitely in both directions.

Question: Define a line segment.
Answer: A line segment is part of a line with two endpoints.

Question: Define a ray.
Answer: A ray has one endpoint and extends infinitely in one direction.

Question: Define a plane.
Answer: A plane is a flat two-dimensional surface extending infinitely in all directions.

Question: Differentiate between intersecting and parallel lines.
Answer: Intersecting lines meet at a point; parallel lines never meet.

Question: Define a triangle.
Answer: A triangle is a polygon with three sides and three angles; sum of angles is 180°.

Question: Classify triangles based on sides.
Answer: Equilateral: all sides equal; Isosceles: two sides equal; Scalene: all sides unequal.

Question: Classify triangles based on angles.
Answer: Acute: all angles < 90°; Right: one angle = 90°; Obtuse: one angle > 90°.

Question: Define a quadrilateral.
Answer: A quadrilateral is a polygon with four sides and four angles.

Question: Define a polygon.
Answer: A polygon is a closed plane figure made of three or more straight line segments.

Question: Differentiate between regular and irregular polygons.
Answer: Regular: all sides and angles equal; Irregular: sides or angles unequal.

Question: Define a circle.
Answer: A circle is a set of points equidistant from a fixed point called the center.

Question: Define radius and diameter.
Answer: Radius: distance from center to circle; Diameter: line passing through center joining two points on circle; diameter = 2×radius.

Question: Define chord and tangent.
Answer: Chord: line segment joining two points on circle; Tangent: line touching circle at exactly one point.

Question: Define concentric circles.
Answer: Two or more circles with same center but different radii.

Answer: obtuse
column6: Acute: <90°; Right: 90°; Obtuse: >90° but <180°; Straight: 180°; Reflex: >180°.

Question: Define complementary and supplementary angles.
Answer: Complementary angles sum to 90°; Supplementary angles sum to 180°.

Question: Define vertically opposite angles.
Answer: Angles opposite each other when two lines intersect; they are equal.

Question: Define adjacent angles.
Answer: Angles with a common vertex and side but do not overlap.

Question: Define a square and its properties.
Answer: Quadrilateral with all sides equal and all angles 90°; diagonals equal and bisect each other at 90°.

Question: Define a rectangle and its properties.
Answer: Quadrilateral with opposite sides equal and all angles 90°; diagonals equal and bisect each other.

Question: Define a parallelogram and its properties.
Answer: Quadrilateral with opposite sides parallel and equal; opposite angles equal; diagonals bisect each other.

Question: Define a rhombus and its properties.
Answer: Quadrilateral with all sides equal; opposite sides parallel; diagonals bisect at right angles and angles.

Question: Define a trapezium and its properties.
Answer: Quadrilateral with one pair of opposite sides parallel; non-parallel sides are called legs.

Question: Explain the sum of interior angles of a polygon.
Answer: Sum = (n−2)×180° for n-sided polygon.

Question: Explain the sum of exterior angles of a polygon.
Answer: Sum of exterior angles = 360°.

Question: Define a diagonal of a polygon.
Answer: A line joining two non-adjacent vertices of a polygon.

Question: Define hypotenuse of a right triangle.
Answer: The side opposite the right angle; it is the longest side.

Question: Explain the triangle inequality.
Answer: Sum of lengths of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the third side.

Question: Define mid-point of a line segment.
Answer: Point dividing a line segment into two equal parts.

Question: Define bisector of a line segment.
Answer: Line or segment dividing a line segment into two equal parts.

Question: Define angle bisector.
Answer: Ray or line that divides an angle into two equal parts.

Question: Explain properties of diagonals of a rectangle.
Answer: Diagonals are equal and bisect each other.

Question: Explain properties of diagonals of a square.
Answer: Diagonals are equal
column3: bisect each other at right angles and bisect angles.

Question: Explain properties of diagonals of a rhombus.
Answer: Diagonals bisect each other at right angles and bisect the angles.

Question: Define secant of a circle.
Answer: A line that intersects a circle at two points.
